summary: A collection of reader reviews for Bob Thomas's biography 'Walt Disney: An American Original.' The reviews highlight Disney's relentless focus on quality over immediate financial return, his pioneering spirit against repeating past successes, and his dedication to continuous learning and giving credit to his team.

principles.statement: Focus entirely on creating the best story and experience; the audience will follow.
principles.rationale: Disney prioritized hiring the right people and starting ambitious projects without letting financial constraints dictate the creative process, trusting that excellence would ultimately attract an audience and generate success.

principles.statement: Give credit to the team behind the creations.
principles.rationale: He recognized that his ambitious ideas required teams of talented people to construct, and he actively wanted to ensure the animators and creators received their due recognition.
principles.source_quote: I want to give credit to the guys who have made these picture for me

principles.statement: Never stop learning and expanding your horizons.
principles.rationale: He believed in continuous education and encouraged it in others, going so far as to co-create a college (CalArts) that allowed students to take classes outside their chosen degrees to broaden their perspectives.

heuristics: You can't top pigs with pigs.

signature_quotes.text: You can't top pigs with pigs.
signature_quotes.context: Coined as a 'no-repeats mantra' after film distributors requested that he make a sequel to the successful '3 Little Pigs' short.

signature_quotes.text: I want to give credit to the guys who have made these picture for me
signature_quotes.context: Spoken to biographer Bob Thomas in 1956 when asking him to write a book about animation.

anti_patterns.description: Chasing easy money by making sequels or repeating past successes.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It stifles the pioneering spirit. Disney believed in always finding the next big challenge rather than relying on the tried-and-true.
